mechanism_narrative: Monthly industrial production momentum captured by INDPRO delivers a persistent directional signal for S&P 500 forward returns because expanding output raises corporate revenue expectations and reduces recession risk premia.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between INDPRO and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the sign of average forward returns contradicts the predicted direction.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating household income and consumption, triggering equity risk-off and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window, or the t-statistic of the return spread between triggered and non-triggered periods is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month growth in retail sales and payrolls captures a synchronized demand expansion that boosts corporate earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns only when both series move together.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and PAYEMS shows a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the conditional forward return when both rise is not significantly higher than when only one rises (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, accelerating consumer prices compress expected profit margins and increase the probability of monetary tightening, causing positive inflation surprises to generate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: In late-cycle months with above-median CPIAUCSL, positive 5-day forward returns follow CPIAUCSL acceleration in more than 55% of instances, or the directional accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present values and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns after month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly positive versus the unconditional mean over any 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 900 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 900, or the mean return in those months exceeds the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with a low unemployment rate indicates capacity constraints and rising wage cost pressures that squeeze corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns when both conditions hold simultaneously.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising INDPRO and UNRATE below 4% fails to produce negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend validates aggregate demand strength and supports upward revisions to sales and earnings estimates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between quarterly GDPC1 changes and 20-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP surprises fail to outperform negative surprises.
